summary_one_line: Input must drive deeper DTD/entity parsing so the dict-backed hash table accumulates a distinct (name,name2,name3) triple, reaching line 580's false branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed DOCTYPE/DTD payload that parses far enough to insert a genuinely new (name,name2,name3) hash triple via xmlHashAddEntry3 on the dict path (line 571 true), so line 580 takes the false (non-duplicate) side. The structural literals (DOCTYPE, SYSTEM, element/entity names) must survive intact through the parser.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winner seed keeps the structural literals intact (e.g. '<?xml version="1', '<!DOCTYPE ', 'a SYSTEM "dtds/1') and reaches xmlHashAddEntry3 with hitcount 2 on the branch; cmplog's I2SRandReplace can repair byte sequences that must match comparison operands deeper in the parse path, letting more declarations parse and populate the hash table.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Naive resolves only 2/10 and stays blocked 8/10 with hitcount 0 on the branch; without I2S substitution it relies on random havoc to keep the multi-byte structural tokens that gate deeper parsing, so the parse aborts before a distinct triple is inserted.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand into input offsets matching the other, repairing the byte literals that gate deeper DTD parsing; the lineage of the winning seed shows I2S-floor dash rows confirming an I2S contribution.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side condition (reach line 580 false on the dict path with a new triple), but the bytes only need to progressively close the distance to the comparison operands that gate deeper parsing, not match exactly in one shot.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolves 9/10 and stays blocked only 1/10 with hitcount 2 on the branch; its CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback preserves inputs that get progressively closer to matching the byte/string comparisons on the parse path, accumulating partial progress until declarations parse and a hash triple is inserted.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Naive has no CMP_MAP gradient: an input that gets one byte closer to a multi-byte literal earns no new coverage bucket, so partial progress is discarded and naive blocks 8/10 with branch hitcount 0.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming-distance / matching-prefix buckets) rewards inputs that approach the comparison operands gating deeper DTD parsing, building the structural literals incrementally so a new hash triple is eventually inserted.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side condition, but the distinguishing requirement is that xmlHashAddEntry3 is reached via multiple distinct call contexts (entity, attribute, relaxng paths) so context-indexed coverage keeps exploring the parse paths that lead to a new-triple insertion.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 9/10 and blocks 0/10; its CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the same xmlHashAddEntry3 edge reached from different callers (xmlAddEntity, xmlAddSpecialAttr, relaxng) is fresh coverage, keeping inputs that drive deeper distinct parse contexts toward an insertion.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Naive folds all call paths into the same bare edge, so re-reaching xmlHashAddEntry3 from a new caller earns no new coverage; it loses the exploration pressure on the deeper parse contexts and blocks 8/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook records (call-context, edge) pairs) makes each distinct caller-chain reaching xmlHashAddEntry3 new coverage, sustaining exploration of the parse paths that produce a distinct hash triple at line 580.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of the dict-path triple-pointer comparison at hash.c:580 in xmlHashAddEntry3.
The blocked side is the false branch, globally blocked.
The branch is on the dict path (line 571 true).
cmplog wins over naive at this branch via the I2S delta.
cmplog resolves 8/10 with branch hitcount 2 while naive stays at hitcount 0.
The cmplog winner seed keeps the DTD structural literals intact.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes a logged CMP operand into matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile wins over naive via the value_profile delta.
value_profile preserves inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching via CMP_MAP buckets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx wins over naive via the ctx_coverage delta.
naive_ctx folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge from different call paths is distinct coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it

evidence_trail.claim: xmlHashAddEntry3 is reachable from multiple distinct callers (entity, attribute, relaxng paths).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: hop 3  entities.c:xmlAddEntity  (/src/libxml2/entities.c:203-285, calls xmlHashAddEntry at line 277)

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S and relies only on havoc/token mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6405 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 3 --seed baca9f1cf32b3884` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 2 and 3 of the ancestor chain, confirming an I2S contribution under the current build (lower-bound floor signal).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose only deep-parse gate is a multi-byte equality literal showing naive resolving the dict-path new-triple insertion as fast as cmplog/value_profile/naive_ctx (no per-technique divergence).

weakest_evidence_point: The branch itself compares interned dict pointers, not input bytes, so the technique advantage is attributed to upstream parse-path gating rather than to solving this comparison directly, which the byte diff cannot confirm offset-by-offset.
